hypothesis: If AMDEN uses diffusion-based generation with an EGNN backbone and energy-based refinement, and the MEG generation is conditioned on target Young's modulus, then the generated amorphous structures will exhibit RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and energy distributions that closely match those of reference materials across different thermal histories and can be steered to achieve specified modulus values, because the SDE-based denoising (with VE/VP schedules) and HMC refinement enforce physically plausible configurations and enable explicit property conditioning.
